# History & Politics

Topics in the **History & Politics** category of *the year 2012*.

<!-- BEGIN GENERATED: category-index — maintained by build-structure; do not edit by hand -->
| Topic | Summary |
|-------|---------|
| [Queen Elizabeth II Diamond Jubilee](queen-elizabeth-diamond-jubilee-2012.md) | The 60th anniversary of Elizabeth II's 1952 accession, marked by a four-day June bank holiday — a 1,000-boat Thames pageant, a Buckingham Palace concert, and a St. Paul's thanksgiving service — only the second Diamond Jubilee in British history after Victoria's. |
| [Syrian Civil War — 2012 Escalation](syrian-civil-war-escalation-2012.md) | The Syrian uprising became a full-scale civil war in 2012 — marked by the ICRC's July 15 declaration, the start of the battle for Aleppo, and repeated Russia–China vetoes at the UN Security Council. |
| [2012 London Summer Olympics](london-2012-olympics.md) | Multi-sport Games held in London, July 27–August 12; first Olympics where every sport included female competitors and London's third time as host. |
| [London 2012 Olympics Opening Ceremony](london-2012-opening-ceremony.md) | Danny Boyle's four-hour "Isles of Wonder" spectacle on July 27, tracing British history from a pastoral idyll through the Industrial Revolution to the digital age — featuring the NHS tribute, the Queen's James Bond cameo, and Tim Berners-Lee's "This is for everyone" — watched by an estimated 900 million viewers. |
| [2012 London Summer Paralympics](london-2012-paralympics.md) | The Games of the XIV Paralympiad, August 29–September 9; a record 4,237 athletes from 164 nations, 2.7 million tickets sold, and Channel 4's "Superhumans" coverage that reshaped para-sport's public profile. |
| [2012 United States Presidential Election](2012-us-presidential-election.md) | Barack Obama defeated Mitt Romney on November 6, winning 332 electoral votes amid shifting demographic coalitions. |
| [Mitt Romney 2012 Presidential Campaign](mitt-romney-2012-campaign.md) | The Republican nominee's run for the presidency — clinching the nomination in May, selecting Paul Ryan, and losing to Obama on November 6 (206 to 332 electoral votes); the leaked "47 percent" video and the Election-Day failure of the ORCA system became defining moments. |
| [Rio 2016 Olympic Bid Success](rio-2016-olympic-bid.md) | The IOC's October 2, 2009 selection of Rio de Janeiro to host the 2016 Summer Olympics — the first in South America — over Madrid, Tokyo, and a first-round-eliminated Chicago; the August 2012 London handover began Rio's four-year countdown, before the Games were shadowed by recession and the later Nuzman bid-bribery conviction. |
| [South Korea 2012 Presidential Election](south-korea-2012-presidential-election.md) | The December 19 election of Park Geun-hye (Saenuri Party) with 51.55% of the vote — the first female South Korean president and daughter of former ruler Park Chung-hee — over Moon Jae-in, on a 75.8% turnout later clouded by the NIS interference scandal and her 2017 impeachment. |
| [2012 US Fiscal Cliff and Congressional Negotiations](us-fiscal-cliff-2012.md) | The convergence of expiring Bush-era tax cuts and Budget Control Act sequestration set for January 1, 2013 — a ~$607 billion contraction (≈4% of GDP) — resolved in December–January brinkmanship by the American Taxpayer Relief Act, which the 89–8 Senate and 257–167 House passed and Obama signed on January 2, 2013. |
<!-- END GENERATED: category-index -->
